name: papaver somniferum
intensity: High and dose-dependent due to opioid alkaloid content.
mechanism: Opioid alkaloids from this species act primarily at opioid receptors with dose-dependent analgesic, sedative, and respiratory effects.
safetyNotes: High-risk profile includes dependence, overdose, and respiratory depression potential.
analgesic and sedative. Herbs often act via neurotransmitter modulation

or enzyme inhibition.. Source of opiates
description: Papaver somniferum is the botanical source of opiate alkaloids associated with analgesic and sedative pharmacology and substantial dependence risk.
legalStatus: Strictly regulated; illegal to grow for opium in most countries
preparation: Preparation and legal status vary widely; concentrated or unregulated products materially increase harm risk.
region: Originally from the Mediterranean and western Asia, now cultivated in multiple regulated regions.
summary: This plant has strong opioid-related pharmacology and safety liabilities; non-medical use carries high overdose and dependence risk.
